info.title: Identity Platform API
info.description: Wire-level API contract for the Identity Platform. See the [semantic contracts](https://github.com/swalusimbi/identity-platform/tree/main/docs/contracts), [getting-started guide](https://github.com/swalusimbi/identity-platform/blob/main/docs/getting-started.md) and [security policy](https://github.com/swalusimbi/identity-platform/blob/main/SECURITY.md).

externalDocs.description: Identity Platform contracts and integration guides

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: User registration, login and token rotation.

tags.name: OAuth
tags.description: Provider login initiation, callbacks and code exchange.

tags.name: Verification
tags.description: Remote verification for JWTs and machine credentials.

tags.name: Roles
tags.description: Role and permission management within one client.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Provision and manage users within one client.

tags.name: API keys
tags.description: Scoped machine credentials owned by one client.

tags.name: Service accounts
tags.description: Role-bearing machine identities and their credentials.

tags.name: JWKS
tags.description: Public signing keys for local JWT verification.

Cache-Control.description: Public cache policy for the key set

post.description: Creates a user inside the client identified by `clientId` and returns the first access and refresh token pair.

201.description: User registered with a token pair

post.summary: Log in with email and password
post.description: Authenticates a user within one client and returns a client-bound token pair.
Rotates the token atomically. Reuse the same `operationId` only when retrying an operation whose response was not received.

200.description: Refresh token revoked when it exists for the client
Uniform response regardless of account existence

post.summary: Reset a password with an emailed token

200.description: Uniform response regardless of account existence

post.summary: Verify an email with an emailed token

parameters.name: code_challenge
parameters.description: PKCE S256 challenge. Provide with code_challenge_method or omit both.

parameters.name: code_challenge_method
parameters.description: Must be S256 and paired with code_challenge. A lone value is rejected.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Consumer one-time value, echoed back on the callback redirect

302.description: Redirect to the provider authorization URL
Redirect to the registered application callback

post.description: Failed client, redirect URI or PKCE checks do not consume the authorization code. A successful exchange consumes it atomically.

post.summary: Verify a JWT or API key remotely
post.description: Checks credential validity, application audience and an optional required permission.

400.description: The expected audience or credential was not provided

get.summary: List the signed-in user's active sessions

delete.summary: Revoke all active sessions for the signed-in user
Revoke one session for the signed-in user

get.summary: List roles for the authenticated client

post.summary: Assign a role to a user
Revoke a role from a user

get.summary: List users for the authenticated client
List API keys for the authenticated client

post.description: Creates a scoped client credential. The complete key is returned once and cannot be retrieved later.

201.description: API key created. The raw key is shown once.

get.summary: List service accounts for the authenticated client

post.description: Creates a machine identity whose permissions are resolved live from its assigned roles.

post.summary: Assign a role to a service account

delete.summary: Revoke a role from a service account

post.summary: Create an API key credential for a service account
post.description: Creates a credential for the service account. The complete key is returned once and carries no independent scopes.

201.description: Service account key created. The raw key is shown once.

get.summary: List audit log entries for the authenticated client

post.description: Registers a public or confidential application. A confidential client secret is returned once.

201.description: Client created. Confidential client secrets are shown once.

200.description: Client secret rotated and shown once

post.summary: Bootstrap a tenant and invite the first admin
post.description: Creates the initial management permissions, administrator role and invited administrator for one client.

ErrorResponse.description: Stable error envelope returned by API failures.

RegisterRequest.description: Registration credentials and the client that will own the user.

LoginRequest.description: User credentials scoped to one application client.

RefreshRequest.description: Refresh credential and retry identity for one atomic rotation operation.

operationId.description: Fresh random UUID for a new operation, reused only for an ambiguous retry

OAuthTokenRequest.description: One-time OAuth code exchange parameters for a public or confidential client.

AuthTokenResponse.description: Client-bound access and refresh credentials issued for a user.

RefreshResponse.description: Replacement credentials issued after refresh token rotation.

VerifyRequest.description: One JWT or API key plus the application that intends to accept it.

audience.description: External client ID of the application accepting the credential

VerifyResponse.description: Credential validity and optional permission decision.

ServiceAccount.description: A non-human principal whose permissions come from assigned roles.

CreateServiceAccountRequest.description: Creates a service account and optionally assigns existing client roles.

CreateServiceAccountKeyRequest.description: Creates a credential for the service account. Its permissions resolve from the account's roles at request time.

CreateServiceAccountKeyResponse.description: A newly created service account key. The full key is returned once.

example.name: billing-worker-production
example.warning: Store this key securely. It cannot be retrieved again.

scopes.description: Service account keys do not carry scopes. Permissions come from the account's roles.

CreateApiKeyRequest.description: Creates a standalone API key with a fixed set of permission scopes.

CreateApiKeyResponse.description: A newly created standalone API key. The full key is returned once.

example.name: reporting-script
example.warning: Store this key securely. It cannot be retrieved again.

CreateClientRequest.description: Registers an application that uses the identity platform.

CreateClientResponse.description: A registered application. Confidential clients receive their secret once.

example.name: Example Web App
example.warning: Store the client secret securely. It cannot be retrieved again.

BootstrapClientRequest.description: Creates the first administrator and role for a client that has no users.

BootstrapClientResponse.description: The initial administrator, assigned role and resulting permissions.

example.message: Admin invited. The emailed link sets their password.
